note: The eng-lead reads this to pick the next task (highest-priority status:ready). The 'real-data wiring' initiative (P1-P22) replaces all mocked/hardcoded frontend data with real DB-backed data. Full detail: specs/real-data-wiring-plan.md. Mirror of the Looper - Product Board. Downstream items are status:blocked until their depends_on ships; the eng-lead flips them to ready as deps land. type: minor=silent (backend/infra/tests, no owner ping) | major=noticeable (user-visible on TestFlight, rolls into an approval bundle).

items.title: Wire Alembic + migration 005 core scoring schema + ORM (Postgres/RDS)
items.why: No relational tables exist for the core scoring domain (players/rounds/scores/tournaments live in JSON). Unblocks the whole initiative. RDS is already up (DATABASE_URL on EC2 + Secrets Manager); set up Alembic, baseline caddie schema 001-004, then author 005 as the first revision; apply on the EC2 deploy box.

items.title: Align frontend lib/api.ts to the real backend contract (kill silent localStorage fallback)
items.why: api.ts uses snake_case + PATCH + nonexistent endpoints, so storage-api.ts always silently falls back to localStorage. Until this lands, backend fixes are invisible in the app.

items.title: Players CRUD on Postgres (drop seed_default_data)
Rounds + normalized scores/players/groups on Postgres
Tournaments on Postgres (round links survive restart)
Scoring-courses on Postgres (resolve course-identity duality)
GET/POST/PUT /api/profile/golfer backed by new golfer_profiles table
items.why: Decision: new golfer_profiles table, distinct from caddie player_profiles.

items.title: Games in normalized games table, round/tournament-scoped (no standalone /api/games)
items.resolution: Satisfied by backend-rounds-scores-db + backend-tournaments-db + api-contract-align: normalized games table (mig 005), round/tournament-scoped CRUD via those endpoints, Game.teams/roundId parity added, and standalone /api/games client funcs removed. No separate endpoint needed (decision E4). No build required.
items.why: Decision: normalized games table (FK round/tournament), managed via round/tournament endpoints; delete imagined getGame/createGame from api.ts.

items.title: Backfill data/*.json into Postgres, then retire JSON storage
Round setup: real VoiceRoundSetup/CourseSearch/PlayerAutocomplete -> POST /api/rounds
In-round scoring loads + persists per stroke to backend
Leaderboard computed from real scores via lib/games.ts (drop LB_MOCK)
Home shows real recent rounds + handicap/stats; fix tournament link
Profile masthead + handicap from /api/profile/golfer
Profile 'the bag' from real club distances
Profile strokes-gained / scoring-by-tee / year-log from real rounds
items.rescope_note: RE-SCOPED 2026-06-27: ScoringByTee + YearLog ARE computable from real getRounds() now -> wire them. StrokesGained + FairwayFan need per-shot data (blocked P28) -> replace fabricated mock with HONEST empty states (no fake numbers, owner rule). Removes the last Preview-only mock on profile.
items.done_note: Re-scoped: ScoringByTee (bucketed by tee x holeCount, per-round toPar avg) + Season log now real from getRounds. StrokesGained+FairwayFan merged into one honest ShotAnalytics placeholder at page bottom (no fabricated numbers). Removed contradicting Recent stub. Reviewer SHIP + designer polish-then-ship; 11 findings folded; gates verified incl. vitest 238. players[0]=owner risk tracked as P34.

items.title: Players page -> /api/players (drop storage seeds)
Tournament detail fetched by id + computed standings (delete tournamentData.ts)
Tournament creation persists via API (not localStorage)
Remove 'Load Sample Players' demo seeding from settings
Implement real match-play Nassau scoring (currently stubbed)
items.why: Games engine is isolated and test-covered; match-play Nassau just shows stroke totals today. Buildable independently of the DB work.

items.title: Tee-time REAL integration (phased: deep-links -> provider slots -> auto-booking)
items.why: Owner chose real integration (not a demo). Phase 1 (ready once api-contract-align lands): real course search + GolfNow/course booking deep-links, no gated API. Phase 2 (live slots via GolfNow Affiliate API) and Phase 3 (auto-booking via Lightspeed/foreUP) are blocked on owner-supplied provider credentials.
items.blocked_on_owner: Phases 2-3 only: GolfNow Affiliate & Partner API creds; Lightspeed/foreUP partner creds. Phase 1 needs no creds.

items.title: Voice parser: fix 'for'->4 in regex + 'dbl bogey' everyone-pattern (found by unit tests)
items.why: test-voice-pipeline found two real bugs in parseVoiceScoresLocally: (1) 'for' (STT for four) is in WORD_TO_NUM but missing from the first/second-pass regex alternations, so 'with a for' yields no score; (2) 'everybody dbl bogey' matches the everyone-regex but the value selector checks t.includes('double') not 'dbl', returning par+1 instead of par+2. Fix both + flip the documenting unit tests to assert correct behavior. Noticeable (voice score behavior) -> goes in the next bundle AFTER #51 ships, not folded into the pending approval.

items.title: Restyle GameResults.tsx + GameLeaderboards.tsx to yardage-book (still dark-mode SaaS)
items.why: Designer flagged (games-matchplay-nassau review): these two user-facing game-result components are entirely dark-mode Tailwind (zinc gradients, emerald/amber rank circles, Lucide Trophy, text-white) — a NORTHSTAR violation predating the wiring work. The in-round LeaderboardSheet (what golfers see during play) is already on-paper; these post-round/standings views are not. Restyle to T.* tokens matching the rest of the app. No logic changes.

items.title: Restyle reachable dark components (auth) — most flagged dark files are DEAD/unmounted
items.why: REACHABILITY AUDIT (2026-06-27): of the ~21 dark-Tailwind components, almost all are DEAD CODE / UNMOUNTED legacy from the pre-yardage-book prototype, superseded by the wired screens. Dead subtrees (0 reachable importers): GamesPanel→AddGameModal/VoiceGameSetup; ScoreGrid→HoleScoreModal; CaddieModal→CaddiePanel→CaddieNotesCard/CustomPersonaModal/PinMarkControl/ShotTrackingControl; plus RoundSummary, TournamentGamesPanel, TournamentLeaderboard, VoiceTournamentSetup, GPSMapView, CameraCapture, CourseSearchImport. ONLY genuinely-reachable dark UI = the auth screens (SignInClient, SignUpClient, AuthProvider — real /sign-in /sign-up routes). So restyling the dead ones is moot; restyle ONLY auth here. SEPARATE, BIGGER ISSUE: several FLAGSHIP features are unmounted (no entry point) — AI caddie, OCR scorecard scan, GPS/shot-tracking, rich mid-round games panel. Those need a product decision (re-mount+wire+restyle vs delete) — see unmounted-features-decision. (Note: ScoreGrid/HoleScoreModal were already restyled before this was discovered — harmless, just unused.)
items.grooming_note_2026_06_27: NOT moot. Follow-up reachability grep (2026-06-27): CourseSearch.tsx and PlayerAutocomplete.tsx both have file-header comments confirming they have no dark classes or lucide-react (already clean). Auth screens grep returned no matches — already restyled as noted. TWO reachable files still violate NORTHSTAR (lucide-react icons): SwipeableRow.tsx (used in /players/page.tsx — imports Trash2, AlertTriangle from lucide-react) and settings/page.tsx (imports Trash2 from lucide-react). Fix = replace those icons with inline SVGs matching the existing style. EditGroupsModal has zero importers (confirmed dead). Recommend: keep status READY with narrowed scope: replace lucide icons in SwipeableRow.tsx and settings/page.tsx only.
items.done_note: All REACHABLE render paths now lucide-free: settings + SwipeableRow (pass 1), players/page + NewTournamentRoundClient (pass 2). Remaining lucide importers are all dead (P29 list) or blocked (P28 GPS/caddie cluster) or unimported (AuthButtons) — they get deleted/reworked there, not restyled. Gates green.

Add a calm caddie entry (e.g. a button on RoundPageClient that opens the caddie in a yardage-book sheet), restyle CaddiePanel + CaddieNotesCard + CustomPersonaModal off dark Tailwind to T.*. items.done_note: Lean voice-first CaddieSheet on in-round screen (/caddie/voice + /caddie/recommend). Reviewer (1 bug + 2 nits) + designer (2 blockers + polish) all folded; gates green. GPS panel remains under P28.

Add an entry point (e.g. on the scoring screen or round setup) to scan a paper scorecard -> auto-fill scores via the existing OCR endpoint; restyle CameraCapture (+ CourseSearchImport if part of the flow) to yardage-book.

items.title: Clear ~108 frontend ESLint errors, then make lint a required CI gate
items.why: npm run lint reports ~108 pre-existing errors. Burn down in small behavior-preserving batches; then promote ESLint to a required CI job.
items.acceptance: npm run lint exits 0; ESLint becomes a required CI job.
items.done_note: Root cause was ESLint linting the Capacitor ios/ minified bundle (~2874 false positives) — added ios/** to globalIgnores. Real ~84 problems in src/ fixed (hook refactors, any->types, unescaped entities). lint exit 0/0 problems; tsc/build/238 tests verified by eng-lead.

items.title: Surface confidence + confirm prompt on low-confidence voice parses
items.why: The parser returns a confidence score but the UI never shows it or asks to confirm. VoiceParseResultSchema (schemas.ts:68) has required confidence; backend parse-round-setup (voice_advanced.py:72) already returns confidence in the response but VoiceRoundSetup.tsx ignores it. In-round scoring (ScoreSheet.tsx) has no voice input at all — only a static 'Or say…' hint text with no mic button. V1 scope: (1) amber kicker on round-setup result card when confidence<0.7 or courseName empty; (2) new voice-to-score flow in ScoreSheet with confidence-aware confirm step. Backend parse-scores needs confidence added to VoiceScoreResponse. Reuse T.warningWash/T.warningInk pattern from ScanSheet.
items.done_note: SETUP slice shipped: backend confidence wired into VoiceRoundSetup; calm amber cue (kicker + dashed warningWash course card) when confidence<0.7 or course missing; high-confidence path unchanged; voice-tests 261/261. Reviewer ship + designer polish-then-ship (false-affordance copy fixed, ScanSheet-consistency nits folded). SCORING half split to voice-to-score-confidence (P35).

items.title: Identify the owner by Clerk user id, not players[0] (fix mis-attribution across home + profile)
items.rationale: Reviewer flagged: home (recent scores + scoring avg) and profile (ScoringByTee + Season log) all assume r.players[0] is the owner. Player has no owner flag; array order is just entry order, so a round where the owner is not first-listed attributes another players scores to the owner — a fabricated-fact-by-bug. Pre-existing (3 call sites: page.tsx:40-41,75-77; profile/page.tsx:71,113). Fix all sites together once user-identity lands: map Clerk user -> player (add owner/userId to Player or round-membership), then derive owner totals from that.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: shipped the full ownerPlayerId plumbing on integration/next. BACKEND: migration 0005_008 adds nullable rounds.owner_player_id; ORM + Pydantic Round/RoundCreate carry ownerPlayerId; create_round stores it (defaults to players[0] when omitted); _build_full_round returns it with a first-player fallback for legacy rounds; +2 integration tests (explicit non-first owner round-trips; default-to-first). FRONTEND: Round/RoundCreate types carry ownerPlayerId; new canonical helper lib/round-owner.ts getOwnerPlayerId(round)=ownerPlayerId??players[0] with 4 unit tests; ALL read sites switched off r.players[0] (page.tsx x2, profile/page.tsx x2, profile-stats.ts x3) + stale comments corrected. This centralizes the owner-identity assumption into ONE tested place and makes it correct end-to-end for any round that carries ownerPlayerId. Gates green (fe: lint/tsc/voice265/unit284/build/crash; be: ruff/models/migration-chain; integration tests run in CI Postgres).

items.title: In-round voice-to-score with confidence/confirm (scoring half of P33)
items.rationale: PM found the in-round scoring path has NO voice mic at all and no confidence field. This is net-new: add a real mic in ScoreSheet, a VoiceScoreResponse.confidence field (backend voice.py), and a confirm step (mirror ScanSheet) when low-confidence. Own bundle — deferred from P33 setup slice. See specs/voice-low-confidence-ux.md scoring section.
items.note: Best done AFTER the current massive bundle ships + is validated, since it touches backend. | Owner approved building now (chose voice-to-score over validating-first) 2026-06-27.
items.done_note: Real mic in ScoreSheet (record->Hearing->parse->confirm), confidence cue<0.65, manual digit-wheel untouched. Backend confidence field (no migration). Reviewer fix-then-ship (open-generation guard fixed hot-mic race + stale-confirm wrong-hole apply; range-validate 1-15) + designer polish (44pt mic, animations, surgical amber) all folded. Gates verified: voice-tests 265, vitest 238, build, pytest 138+13skip.

items.title: Backend pytest suite for caddie pure-logic + CI gate
items.done_note: 138 tests (strokes_gained/club_selection/dispersion/aim_point/_safe_json_extract); pytest added to dev deps + required CI step. No bugs found. Verified 138 pass from clean sync.

items.title: Promote backend route/integration tests from advisory to required once first CI run is green
items.rationale: 13 route/integration tests (auth-fails-closed, IDOR, persistence, CRUD) were written without a local Postgres so never executed; currently an advisory continue-on-error CI job. After the first real GitHub Actions run shows the advisory-backend-integration job green, fold it into required-backend (add postgres service there, drop continue-on-error) and delete the advisory job.
items.note: Reverted to advisory 2026-06-28: when required + run with the full suite, the integration harness fails with asyncpg "attached to a different loop" (5 tests). Re-promote after fix-integration-test-loop.
items.done_note: Re-promoted to required after P45 fix: required-backend now runs full suite (138 pure-logic + 13 integration) with Postgres service. 151 tests green in CI.

items.title: Post-round recap screen (calm yardage-book finished-scorecard on round complete)
items.rationale: GAP found in code: handleFinish flips status + dumps straight to home, no recap. New RoundRecap shown on finish (final scores vs par, highlights, game results via existing GameResults); reads existing data, no backend change. Northstar-aligned end-of-round moment.
items.done_note: New RoundRecap on finish (was: dumped straight to home). Final scores vs par, best-hole + birdie/eagle kickers, GameResults reused read-only (added backward-compat readOnly prop). Reviewer SHIP (persistence byte-for-byte intact) + designer polish-then-ship (sticky Done, readOnly games suppress 18 warnings/collapse tables, ROUND COMPLETE anchor) all folded. Gates verified.

items.title: Add Sign Out (missing entirely) + restyle Settings to yardage-book T.*
items.rationale: GAPS found: (1) no signOut anywhere — app gates on sign-in but cannot sign out (AuthButtons dead/unmounted); (2) settings/page.tsx is the only screen still on Tailwind/CSS classes (T.* count 0), a NORTHSTAR inconsistency. Sign-out via Clerk useClerk().signOut() -> AuthGate shows sign-in. Restyle whole page to T.*; keep About + Clear Local Cache.
items.done_note: Sign Out added (was entirely missing — Clerk useClerk().signOut -> AuthGate shows sign-in; mount guarded by same env var as AuthProvider). Settings fully restyled Tailwind->T.* yardage-book. Reviewer ship + designer polish (neutral non-alarming sign-out, in-page confirms for both actions, friendly error, honest Beta version) folded. Gates verified.

items.title: Wire delete-round UI (plumbing existed, no affordance)
items.rationale: GAP: full delete-round stack already built+tested (DELETE /api/rounds/{id} owner-scoped+cascade, api.deleteRound, deleteRoundAsync, useApi.deleteRound) but NO UI calls it — users cannot remove a mistaken/test round. Add swipe-to-delete on home recent-rounds reusing existing SwipeableRow (as players page does). Low-risk: only UI wiring.
items.done_note: Swipe-to-delete on home recent-rounds via existing SwipeableRow + deleteRoundAsync (plumbing pre-existed, no UI). Optimistic removal + date-sorted rollback, liveRoundId cleared, stats refresh. Reviewer SHIP + designer SHIP; 4 consistency nits folded. Known gap (documented): deleteRound/Player swallow API failures.

items.title: Fix voice/auth 401: switch Capacitor iosScheme capacitor->https so Clerk can mint tokens
items.rationale: Owner still hits transcribe 401 (no auth token). Token-bridge code verified correct -> root cause is Clerk failing to issue session JWTs on the custom capacitor://localhost origin. Fix: iosScheme https (https://localhost is Capacitor-recommended, first-class secure origin Clerk supports + secure context for mic). COORDINATED: backend CORS must allow https://localhost (added) and deploy together -> ships with the bundle merge. Cannot be a standalone test build vs current prod (would CORS-block). Gates green. UNVERIFIED on-device (cannot test here); fallback if insufficient = Clerk dashboard allowed-origin / pk_live.

items.title: Playwright auth/login E2E gate (owner QA feedback)
items.done_note: Playwright + @clerk/testing. Tier-1 (sign-in screen renders — would have caught the auth-gate breakage) now RUNS in CI via the public pk_test key (no secret needed). Tier-2 (full sign-in w/ Clerk test user) skips until CLERK_SECRET_KEY repo secret + test user added. Advisory CI job until first green, then promote Tier-1 to required. Limitation: web E2E does NOT reproduce Capacitor webview-origin issues (needs device/simulator smoke).

items.title: Finish Clerk production instance (pk_live) — DNS + key swap, the real login fix
items.rationale: Owner paused mid-setup. Clerk prod domain looperapp.org needs 5 CNAMEs in Route53 (accounts->accounts.clerk.services; clerk->frontend-api.clerk.services; clk._domainkey->dkim1.y38xe4k5qi21.clerk.services; clk2._domainkey->dkim2.y38xe4k5qi21.clerk.services; clkmail->mail.y38xe4k5qi21.clerk.services). looper-loop IAM lacks route53 perms so owner adds them. Then owner sends pk_live + JWKS URL + issuer; eng swaps ship.sh key + backend CLERK_JWKS_URL/ISSUER, redeploys, rebuilds. See specs/clerk-production-migration.md. Permanent login fix; auth-bypass build is the stopgap.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: eng added all 5 Clerk CNAMEs to Route53 (zone Z068252329IRE4BQ9U293) after owner granted route53 perms to looper-loop IAM — all resolve live. Awaiting: owner clicks Verify in Clerk -> sends pk_live + JWKS URL + issuer -> eng swaps ship.sh key + backend CLERK_JWKS_URL/ISSUER, redeploys, rebuilds. | 2026-06-28: owner GO. Deploy points backend at prod Clerk for VERIFICATION only (CLERK_JWKS_URL/ISSUER=clerk.looperapp.org); OWNER_CLERK_USER_ID left untouched (no gate weakening). After owner first sign-in: capture their prod sub -> set OWNER_CLERK_USER_ID to it (P44). | 2026-06-28: PR #52 merged, backend deployed (prod Clerk verification confirmed), pk_live build v1.0.335 (202606280011) uploaded to TestFlight. Awaiting owner sign-in test. If data 403s, set OWNER_CLERK_USER_ID to owner prod sub (P44). | 2026-06-28: "Preparing your book" hang root-caused to prod Clerk rejecting Origin https://localhost (origin_invalid). FIXED via Backend API PATCH /v1/instance allowed_origins=[https://localhost, capacitor://localhost] (HTTP 204; verified /v1/environment now 200 for that origin). No rebuild needed — owner reopens v1.0.335. SECURITY: owner pasted sk_live in chat -> must rotate.

items.title: Fix integration-test event-loop teardown (asyncpg attached to a different loop)
items.rationale: tests/integration fail with RuntimeError: ...attached to a different loop / Event loop is closed when run as part of the full pytest suite. Cause: app.db.engine module-level async engine/async_session is bound to one event loop, but pytest-asyncio (asyncio_mode=auto, function-scoped loop) creates a new loop per test. Fix: create/dispose the engine per test (or per session-scoped loop), e.g. recreate engine in a fixture bound to the running loop, or set a session-scoped event_loop, or dispose engine between tests. Then re-promote integration tests to required (promote-integration-tests).
items.done_note: Session-scoped pytest-asyncio loop fixed the "attached to a different loop" bug. Verified: 13 integration tests pass against Postgres in CI (PR #53).

items.title: Rotate Clerk sk_live (pasted in chat during allowed-origins fix)
items.rationale: Owner pasted sk_live to let eng PATCH allowed_origins. Rotate in Clerk Dashboard -> API Keys. allowed_origins change persists after rotation.

items.title: Clerk session persistence in iOS webview (standardBrowser:false)
items.rationale: Sign-in completed but session did not persist (isSignedIn stayed false -> bounced to signed-out) — iOS WKWebView blocks Clerk third-party session cookie. Fix: standardBrowser:false on ClerkProvider (Clerk official native option, avoids cookies), gated to Capacitor.isNativePlatform() so web/dev unaffected; + CapacitorCookies enabled. WKAppBoundDomains layer REMOVED (risky for Capacitor localhost). Awaiting on-device test.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: shipped v1.0.339 (build 202606280108) with standardBrowser:false + CapacitorCookies (WKAppBoundDomains dropped as too risky for Capacitor). Awaiting owner: sign in -> does session persist (reach home + stay)? | Bundled into COMPLETE native-auth build v1.0.343 (build 202606280228), uploaded; CapacitorPreferences SPM resolved clean. Awaiting owner: sign in -> stays -> force-quit -> still signed in.
items.note: ROOT CAUSE (matches evidence exactly): WebView CORS blocks reading the Authorization response header cross-origin (capacitor://localhost -> clerk.looperapp.org), so the session JWT is never captured (tok never flips). FIX: CapacitorHttp:{enabled:true} routes fetch through native NSURLSession which bypasses browser CORS + reads all headers. + richer copyable diagnostic (auth-hdr field = key signal). Building. WATCH: CapacitorHttp patches global fetch — verify the audio-blob transcribe upload + other API calls still work on-device. | Shipped v1.0.361 (build 202606280650). Awaiting owner: sign in -> auth-hdr/signed true? Copyable diagnostic added. | v1.0.361 diagnostic: native-sent:FALSE (window hooks never fire with CDN clerk-js) — CONFIRMED. Fix: bundle @clerk/clerk-js locally + register FAPI callbacks on the INSTANCE + pass Clerk={instance} to ClerkProvider (the @clerk/expo pattern). Building. | Shipped v1.0.365 (build 202606280936): local @clerk/clerk-js instance + instance callbacks. Awaiting owner: native-sent should now be true. | CRASH ROOT CAUSE: bundled Clerk instance had no UI components -> <SignIn> mountSignIn assertComponentsReady throws -> white-screen. FIXED (d78de56): standard CDN ClerkProvider + guarded window hooks; app loads (sim-verified). NATIVE-AUTH ROOT CAUSE: clerk-react@5/clerk-js@5 dont honor the token hooks -> native-sent never fires. REAL FIX: upgrade clerk-react->v6 (clerk-js@6 honors hooks + ships UI). Building v6 upgrade. Autonomous testing now exists: frontend/ios/SIMTEST.md + simtest-headless.mjs (CI-able crash check). | VERIFIED 2026-06-28 via REAL credentialed sign-in in iPhone-17 sim (WebKit remote inspector + ios-webkit-debug-proxy): @clerk/react 6.11.1 / clerk-js 6.22.0. ALL native-auth signals GREEN: native-sent=true on EVERY FAPI request incl. sign_ins POST/attempt_first_factor (v6 honors window hooks); auth-hdr=true + tok=true (CapacitorHttp made authorization response header readable, JWT captured + persisted to Preferences); napi=true. Password (first factor) ACCEPTED. signed=false reached ONLY because Clerk gated the new device behind an emailed second-factor OTP ('signing in from a new device') which the agent can't read (no inbox) — Clerk product security, orthogonal to native-auth plumbing. CONCLUSION: native-auth fix CONFIRMED working; remaining step is the human entering the email OTP on first sign-in. Shipped verified build v1.0.369 (build 202606281037).

items.title: Persist Clerk session across cold restart (tokenCache via @capacitor/preferences)
items.rationale: With standardBrowser:false + no tokenCache, the session is in-memory only — force-quit loses it. Add a tokenCache backed by @capacitor/preferences so sign-in survives app restarts. Follow-up after the native-session fix is confirmed working.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: implemented via Clerk internal __internal_onBeforeRequest/onAfterResponse hooks + @capacitor/preferences (native Keychain), key __clerk_client_jwt (same as @clerk/expo). Gated to native. Bundled into the complete native-auth build with standardBrowser:false. | Bundled into COMPLETE native-auth build v1.0.343 (build 202606280228), uploaded; CapacitorPreferences SPM resolved clean. Awaiting owner: sign in -> stays -> force-quit -> still signed in.

items.title: Auth-storage hardening: Keychain for Clerk JWT + clear-on-signout + fix false comments
items.rationale: Security review of v1.0.343 (auth wiring otherwise SOUND). Three items, bundle as one pass before production: (1) store __clerk_client_jwt in a Keychain-backed plugin (@aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage or capacitor-secure-storage-plugin) with kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so it is excluded from device/iCloud backups (currently @capacitor/preferences = UserDefaults plaintext, backup-exposed — Medium); (2) the AuthProvider comments at :50 and :117-118 + the commit msg FALSELY claim Keychain — correct them; (3) on Clerk sign-out, Preferences.remove/secureStorage.remove the JWT (hooks currently never clear it -> stale credential persists after sign-out). Also confirm Clerk FAPI returns Access-Control-Expose-Headers: Authorization for the native flow (load-bearing for the response-header read). Not beta-blocking (private TestFlight, owner-only); REQUIRED before App Store/wider release.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: shipped on integration/next — (2) corrected the FALSE "Keychain" comments in AuthProvider; (3) clear-on-signout: ClerkTokenBridge now clears the persisted native JWT on a real signed-in->signed-out transition (ref-guarded so cold-start restoration is never clobbered; native-only), fixing the stale-credential-after-signout vuln; + centralized all token read/write/clear into frontend/src/lib/native-token-store.ts (one place to swap the backend) with a unit test (4 cases). Sub-item "confirm FAPI exposes Authorization header for native flow" = CONFIRMED (credentialed sim test showed auth-hdr=true via CapacitorHttp). REMAINING part (1) Keychain plugin swap split to clerk-jwt-keychain-swap (needs on-device Keychain verification).

items.title: Profile stats: par-type breakdown + score distribution + trend
items.done_note: 3 pure helpers (deriveParTypeAverages/ScoreDistribution/Trend) + 38 unit tests; 2 yardage-book sections from existing rounds. Designer polish-then-ship, all 5 fixes folded. Frontend-only, gates green (276 tests). Behind login like all features.

items.title: Graceful offline/error degradation on home + in-round (on-course resilience)
items.done_note: Home: navigator.onLine-driven offline note (was dead via swallowed errors), loading skeleton, error+retry, try/catch/finally. In-round: load-fail retry, red->amber banners (score is locally safe), refined messaging; seq-guard/optimistic/offline logic untouched (reviewer SHIP). Leaderboard already resilient (no change). Designer polish + reviewer nits folded; a11y roles added. Gates green (276 tests).

items.title: Enable "Native Applications" in Clerk dashboard (native_api_disabled — the session blocker)
items.rationale: Deep-fix found native mode fails with native_api_disabled until Native Applications is enabled in Clerk: https://dashboard.clerk.com/last-active?path=native-applications -> Configure -> Native applications -> Enable. One-time, no rebuild. Combined with the code fix (x-mobile + always-set auth header) this should make the session persist.

items.title: Wire simtest-headless.mjs (client-side crash check) into CI
items.rationale: New harness frontend/ios/simtest-headless.mjs runs the prod bundle through Chromium w/ faked iOS bridge, exits non-zero on client-side crash (would have caught the v1.0.365 white-screen). Add as a CI step so client-side crashes are caught automatically (owner QA-feedback aligned).
items.progress: 2026-06-28: wired into CI required-frontend job — build now bakes the public prod Clerk pk so the native auth path executes, then `npm run test:native-crash` (ios/simtest-headless.mjs) runs the prod bundle in Chromium with the iOS bridge faked and fails the build on any client-side exception. Verified locally: healthy build exits 0 (loaded=true, native-sent=true, no PAGEERROR); CORS errors to clerk.looperapp.org are expected requestfailed (no native bridge headlessly) and correctly do NOT count as a crash. Catches the v1.0.365 white-screen class automatically.

items.title: Swap native JWT storage backend from UserDefaults to Keychain plugin
items.rationale: Final part of clerk-jwt-keychain. Replace @capacitor/preferences (UserDefaults, plaintext, backup-exposed) with @aparajita/capacitor-secure-storage using kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly so the Clerk JWT is encrypted at rest + excluded from device/iCloud backups. Thanks to native-token-store.ts this is a single-file backend swap (+ add the plugin + cap sync). NOTE: adds a native dependency (SPM/pod) and the actual Keychain persistence can only be fully verified on a real device/sim sign-in -> bundle with other device-verifiable changes or verify via a credentialed sim run before shipping. REQUIRED before App Store / wider release; NOT beta-blocking (owner-only TestFlight).
review_followups: LOW (TOCTOU, reviewer 2026-06-28): an in-flight FAPI response can re-persist the JWT just after clearNativeToken() on sign-out. Security-nil (Clerk destroys the session server-side; re-persisted token is revoked). Fix when doing the Keychain swap: gate setNativeToken on a module-level 'signed out' flag that clearNativeToken sets and a sign-in transition (false->true) resets — taking care NOT to suppress the re-sign-in token write (it happens during the sign_in FAPI exchange, before isSignedIn flips).

LOW (cold-start stale token, reviewer 2026-06-28): if sign-out happens while the app is killed, the stale (already server-invalid) JWT isn't cleared until it's injected and rejected. Fix: in nativeOnAfterResponse, clearNativeToken() on a FAPI authentication-failure error code — but first confirm the EXACT Clerk FAPI error code via a credentialed run so a valid token is never wrongly wiped.

items.title: Round setup: let the owner mark which player is "me" (sets ownerPlayerId)
items.rationale: Completes owner-player-identity. The backend + read sites now honour round.ownerPlayerId, but round/new (frontend/src/app/round/new/page.tsx) still sends players in form order with no owner designation, so ownerPlayerId always defaults to players[0]. Add a calm, yardage-book-styled way to mark exactly one player row as "you" (default the first), pre-filling the owner's Clerk name (useUser) is optional. Pass ownerPlayerId in createRound. THIS is the change that actually fixes mis-attribution when the owner is not first-listed. Needs designer review (user-facing setup UX).
items.progress: 2026-06-28: shipped on integration/next. round/new now lets the owner mark which player is "you" — ownerIndex state (default first), a quiet serif-italic "you" marker on the owner row, and a "✓ this is me" pill in the player editor; ownerPlayerId resolved at create (handles id reassignment + dedup; falls back to first). Designer-reviewed (yardage-book): ship-blocker fixed (pill moved ABOVE the autocomplete so the inline suggestion can not push it off-sheet while typing) + typography moved to serif-italic lowercase to match the "you" annotation register; default-checked-on-Player-1 and T.pencil "you" marker approved as-is. Gates: lint/tsc/voice265/unit284/build/native-crash. This makes the owner-player-identity fix USER-VISIBLE: a round where the owner is not first-listed now attributes the owner’s own scores correctly in home + profile.

items.title: Voice score confirm: name exactly which players a low-confidence parse missed
items.rationale: Voice is the Northstar's primary interface. The score-confirm panel showed only a generic 'double-check these' + amber on absent tiles, so the golfer had to infer which name to fix. Now, on a LOW-confidence parse that missed some (not all) players, a calm note names them.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: shipped on integration/next. New pure helper lib/voice/confirm-guidance.ts (missingScoreNote + joinNames + missingPlayerNames) gated to confidence<0.65 AND some-but-not-all players missing (intentional one-score dictation and empty parses are NOT nagged); +10 unit tests. Rendered as a quiet serif-italic line in VoiceConfirmPanel (ScoreSheet.tsx). Designer-reviewed: ship-blocker fixed (note color amber->T.pencil so it's a calm aside, not a 3rd amber layer) + wording trimmed to just naming who was missed (kept minimal per Northstar). NOTE: backend /api/voice/parse-scores + local fallback only return {scores,confidence} (no warnings/explanations on the SCORE path — those exist only in the round-SETUP pipeline), so this derives the note deterministically client-side. Gates: lint/tsc/voice265/unit294/build/native-crash.

items.title: Agentic conversational round setup — caddie asks back for missing info
items.rationale: OWNER PRIORITY (2026-06-28): make voice setup a back-and-forth agent, not a one-shot parser. If the golfer says who's playing but not the course, the caddie should ASK ('Which course? Which tees?') and fill the gaps over multiple turns, instead of failing with 'no course matched'. Turns voice from parse-or-fail into a conversation that always reaches a complete, startable round. Likely: multi-turn state in VoiceRoundSetup + a backend prompt that returns {filled fields, missing fields, a spoken follow-up question}; loop until complete. High priority.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: BACKEND foundation shipped on integration/next. parse-round-setup is now multi-turn: request carries current+expecting, response returns missing/followUpQuestion/complete; pure helpers (round_setup_missing, round_setup_question, merge_round_setup, _finalize_round_setup) + expecting-biased local fallback; 18 unit tests; backward compatible. REMAINING (next cycle, top priority): wire VoiceRoundSetup to the loop — on an incomplete parse, show the caddie question and auto-record the answer (pass current+expecting), looping until complete, with a max-turn guard; auto-parse after transcription for a smooth single-tap conversation. That part is device-verifiable (live voice I/O) + needs designer review, so it gets its own focused cycle. Extract a pure nextConvoStep reducer for unit tests. | 2026-06-28: FRONTEND loop shipped on integration/next. VoiceRoundSetup is now multi-turn: nextConvoStep reducer (+8 tests, MAX 4 follow-ups), handleParse sends current+expecting and branches complete/ask, auto-parse (no manual tap), caddie question in header. Reviewer-fixed (mountedRef mic-leak guard; per-turn parse-guard reset) + designer-fixed (question in header, removed dead transcribed phase/Understand-this button, gentler wording). Gates green. NOTE: end-to-end conversation requires the new parse-round-setup on PROD (rides the bundle merge -> deploy); against the old prod endpoint nextConvoStep degrades to one-shot (no fields -> complete). | 2026-06-28: REALTIME pivot started. Backend foundation: round-less POST /api/realtime/setup-session (no existing round needed) + set_round_setup tool (course, players with PER-PLAYER tee, default tee, holes, gameFormat) + conversational setup instructions (caddie/setup_voice.py); reuses mint_ephemeral_session (whisper-1 live transcription + server VAD). 6 unit tests. NEXT (device-tested): rewire VoiceRoundSetup to the realtime client (reuse realtime.ts/useRealtimeCaddie) — live transcription + the caddie asking back + dispatch set_round_setup -> createRound. Replaces the batch record/transcribe/parse flow. | 2026-06-28: FRONTEND Realtime integration done. startSetupSession (api) + realtime.ts setup mode (round-less mint + set_round_setup ack) + new VoiceRoundSetupRealtime.tsx (continuous Realtime session: live transcription, caddie talks back, conversation UI, mute, error/retry) wired into round/new (replaces the batch VoiceRoundSetup). On set_round_setup tool -> maps to handleVoiceSetup (fills course/players/tee, user confirms with Tee off). Gates: tsc/lint/voice265/unit304/build/native-crash. WebRTC voice path is DEVICE-ONLY verifiable -> needs a TestFlight test. Caveat: per-player tee groups are captured in the tool but the round model carries a single tee for now (default = first player tee) -> see voice-per-player-tees follow-up.

items.title: Live (real-time) transcription on device while recording
items.rationale: OWNER PRIORITY (2026-06-28): show words as they're spoken. Today VoiceRoundSetup runs Web Speech API for interim text (startListening, VoiceRoundSetup.tsx ~142) but that API is unavailable in the iOS WKWebView, so nothing shows on device. Need real streaming STT (e.g. Deepgram live WebSocket) feeding interim text to the existing interimTranscript UI, with the current record->stop->Deepgram batch as fallback. Apply to ScoreSheet voice + VoiceRoundSetup. High priority.

items.title: Mic button on the right + single-tap opens AND starts recording
items.rationale: OWNER simplification ask (2026-06-28): mic was bottom-LEFT and required two taps (open overlay, then tap mic). Moved mic to the RIGHT of the New Round footer (order:2) and added autoStart to VoiceRoundSetup so a single tap opens the overlay and begins recording immediately.

items.title: EPIC: Map/GPS + caddie intelligence (plays-like, DECADE-style management)
items.rationale: Owner strategic direction (2026-06-28). Caddie that gives plays-like yardages + 'hit this club' using contours/slope + weather/wind, plus a DECADE-style expected-strokes course-management optimizer we build ourselves. Strategy: LICENSE the commodity layer (catalog, scorecard, F/C/B points, hazard/green polygons), BUILD the terrain intelligence (USGS 3DEP/NAIP, free) + the management engine + clean API; LICENSE-OR-SKIP green-reading (needs survey-grade data). KEY FINDING from planning: most caddie math ALREADY EXISTS (backend/app/caddie/strokes_gained.py, dispersion.py, club_selection.py plays-like, services/weather.py, services/elevation.py) — gaps are a true optimizer, a 3DEP precompute pipeline, tactical slope advice, and on-course UX. See phased cards tagged epic=map-gps-caddie-intelligence.

items.title: Plays-like yardage card in the caddie panel
items.rationale: Surface target_yards ('plays like') vs raw_yards + the per-factor adjustments breakdown ALREADY returned by /recommend, plus a wind-relative-to-shot chip. CaddiePanel.tsx. Data already present; mostly a UI surfacing win. Component test headless; live GPS = device.

items.title: Finish altitude→pressure in air-density (weather.py:73 TODO)
items.rationale: Implement ~12 hPa/100m altitude→pressure so high-altitude plays-like/air-density is correct; add unit test. Fully self-verifiable.
items.progress: 2026-06-28: investigated — the TODO was moot: the caller passes Open-Meteo surface_pressure (already altitude-adjusted), so applying a 12hPa/100m term would double-count and overstate thin-air at altitude. Corrected the misleading comment + added 5 unit tests verifying direction (standard~1, Denver-alt<0.9, cold/dry>1, hot/humid<1, no double-count).

items.title: Competition-legal mode toggle
items.rationale: competition_legal flag on recommend/session that zeroes elevation/wind/slope adjustments and tags the recommendation (USGA device legality); UI switch. Backend self-verifiable; legal-correctness sensitive.

items.title: Feed licensed hazard/green polygons into hole intelligence
items.rationale: course_intel.build_hole_intelligence should consume hole_features polygons (water/bunker/ob) from PostGIS, preferring them over OSM; compute side+distance from geometry. Better inputs for everything downstream. Needs a polygon data source (license eval).

items.title: DECADE expected-strokes target optimizer (dispersion convolution core)
items.rationale: New caddie/target_optimizer.py: over candidate aim points, sample the player's dispersion ellipse (dispersion.get_dispersion) across an expected-strokes field (strokes_gained.expected_strokes + hazard penalties); return the target minimizing expected strokes. Pure, unit-testable. THE differentiator — high value, low device risk. Calibration is the risk (keep params tunable).

items.title: Wire DECADE optimizer into generate_recommendation
items.rationale: Replace/augment the heuristic aim point in aim_point.py with the optimizer's target + expected-strokes delta vs aim-at-flag; keep traffic-light fallback when geometry missing. Gate behind a flag; snapshot tests to catch regressions.

items.title: Tactical slope/miss-bias advice ('bigger slope long → miss short, club down')
items.rationale: Use green/approach slope + hazard asymmetry so the engine emits DECADE-style lines and biases select_club + miss-side. Unit-test the decision, not the prose. This is the owner's headline 'club down' tactic.

items.title: Personalized dispersion & skill profile from logged shots
items.rationale: Extend caddie/learning.py to derive per-club dispersion + miss bias from tracked shots, persisted on PlayerProfile (mirror personal_sg); get_dispersion prefers personal over table. Cold-start fallback to tables.

items.title: USGS 3DEP COG ingestion pipeline (per course)
items.rationale: Offline job: fetch 3DEP 1m bare-earth DEM for a course bbox, build a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, upload to S3; read via rasterio/rio-tiler (windowed). Add rasterio/rio-tiler/boto3. Pilot on a handful of courses. First real heavy lift. Risks: CRS/data gaps/S3 cost.

items.title: Per-hole elevation profile precompute + store
items.rationale: Sample tee→green line + green grid from the COG into a hole_elevation_profiles table; replaces live USGS EPQS for profiled courses (EPQS stays fallback). Enables cheap plays-like lookups.

items.title: Target-point plays-like (any aim point, not just green center)
items.rationale: Cheap elevation lookup for an arbitrary aim/target lat-lng from the profile/COG so plays-like is correct for layups and the optimizer's chosen target. Cache windowed reads for latency.

items.title: Slope severity along the shot line for tactical advice
items.rationale: Feed approach-zone slope (from the profile) into the tactical-advice engine so 'bigger slope there' is data-driven, not green-only. Depends on dem-hole-profiles + caddie-tactical-slope-advice.

items.title: Tap-to-target on the map → live plays-like + recommendation
items.rationale: Add aim-point selection in GPSMapView.tsx; on tap, compute bearing/distance and call recommend with the target so plays-like + optimizer target render on the map. Device-tested (map/GPS).

items.title: Migrate GPS to @capacitor/geolocation (web fallback)
items.rationale: gps.ts currently uses navigator.geolocation though @capacitor/geolocation is installed; swap with web fallback + permissions for better iOS accuracy/background. Owner device test.

items.title: Voice tactical advice (speak the plays-like number + 'club down')
items.rationale: Surface optimizer target + slope/miss lines through /session/voice context so the spoken caddie says the plays-like number and the tactic. Builds on the conversational caddie.

items.title: Wind-direction-relative visualization
items.rationale: Show head/cross/tail relative to the chosen shot bearing on the map/panel using existing compute_wind_adjustment output.

items.title: Nationwide DEM/NAIP automation + optional ML feature extraction
items.rationale: Generalize the COG pipeline to many courses; optionally use NAIP to auto-extract fairway/green/hazard polygons where licensed feeds lack them. NEVER derive from Google/Mapbox imagery. Defer until pilots proven. Cost/ML-accuracy/ops risk.

items.title: On-device offline plays-like blob
items.rationale: Ship per-round elevation-profile JSON to the device (Capacitor Preferences) for offline lookup on-course. Device-tested.

items.title: Green-reading / putt-break (LICENSE or SKIP)
items.rationale: Integrate a survey-grade green-contour vendor (e.g. iGolf Green Heat Maps) for putt break. Do NOT derive from 1m DEM (physical resolution ceiling). Gated on an owner licensing decision; out of scope until decided.

items.title: Fix /tournament/<id> deep-link (same static-export hard-reload bug as rounds)
items.rationale: tournament/[id] uses the same generateStaticParams placeholder pattern as round/[id], so opening a tournament hard-reloads → Capacitor index.html shell → AuthGate 'Preparing your book' stuck screen. Apply the same fix as the round-nav fix: a static /tournament/view?id= route + read id from useSearchParams + roundHref-style helper + update tournament nav sites. Verified pattern in the round fix.

items.title: Per-player tee groups (data model + scoring) for 'Dan & I blues, Matt & John whites'
items.rationale: The Realtime set_round_setup tool captures per-player tees, but Round/Player carry a single teeName. To honor split tees end-to-end, add per-player tee to the Player/round model + UI + handicap/scoring implications, and map the tool's players[].tee through. Until then the agent uses a single default tee (first player's).

items.title: VoiceRoundSetupRealtime polish: drag-to-dismiss, mic icon, live header subtitle
items.rationale: Designer nice-to-haves deferred from the first build (ship-blockers already fixed): (8) add drag-to-dismiss like the caddie sheet; (5) replace 'MUTE'/'MUTED' text with the mic SVG + strikethrough; (7) swap the static 'Tell me what you're playing.' subtitle to the live status once a conversation is active. Also apply the 44pt close-target fix to the old VoiceRoundSetup.tsx if it's still reachable.

items.title: Surface Playing Partners (unhide the orphaned /players roster)
items.rationale: The full SavedPlayer roster CRUD already exists at /players but is linked from nowhere. Rename/restyle to 'Playing Partners' (yardage-book), add two quiet entry points: home masthead/profile link + inline in the round-setup & tee-time player pickers. No backend. NO bottom tab bar (see ui_decision). First, lowest-risk slice of the social epic.

items.title: Partner profile detail (/players/[id])
items.rationale: Read-only partner profile: name, handicap, rounds played together (roundsPlayed), recent shared rounds. Reuses GET /api/players/{id}. Pushed detail route, not a tab. Optional shared-rounds aggregation backend.

items.headless: partial (UI yes; SMS share needs device)
items.title: In-app invite surfaces (wire the existing '+ Invite')
items.rationale: From round-setup, tee-time (tee-time/page.tsx:165) and add-players, pick a partner and invite; deliver in-app + native share/SMS link. New RoundInvite table (owner-scoped first). Real recipients arrive with the friend graph (A4).

items.headless: NO (needs 2 real accounts — owner device test)
items.title: Real cross-account friend graph (Friendship model + relax owner-only gate)
items.rationale: Friendship table + endpoints; link by clerk_user_id; accept/decline. CRITICAL: the app is single-owner gated — main.py applies require_owner to EVERY router. Real social requires relaxing this for new social routes (use current_user_id with per-row authz). This gate change is the security-review hotspot — MUST pass /security-review + /code-review. Gates A5/A6. DECISION NEEDED from owner: open the app beyond owner-only?

items.title: Phone-number friend matching (privacy-preserving)
items.rationale: Capacitor contacts plugin (net-new) + explicit consent screen + client-side E.164 normalize + HMAC-SHA256 hash (never upload raw numbers) → POST /api/partners/match against hashes of opted-in users. Per-user 'discoverable by phone' toggle (default off). NSContactsUsageDescription + Apple privacy manifest. Privacy/legal (TCPA) — highest-risk card.

items.title: Push-delivered invites (rare-by-design)
items.rationale: Capacitor push plugin + APNs; push ONLY for accepted/tee-time-found invite moments (strict trigger list — notifications are rare by design per CLAUDE.md). Device-token store + send. Do last.

items.title: Course detail screen + 'Start a round here' (/courses/[id])
items.rationale: Extract the existing CourseSearch/golf-api.ts flow into a /courses route + detail screen; 'Start a round here' reuses round/new onSelectCourse (already works). One quiet new spoke from home — a route, NOT a tab. Pin id source to golfApiCourseId.

items.title: CourseReview model + write-a-review after a round
items.rationale: CourseReview table keyed on golfApiCourseId (sidesteps the course-table unification refactor). Review entry from the RoundRecap modal (RoundPageClient.tsx:612) — the natural 'review the course' moment. Rating + short note. Fall back to course name if no golfApiCourseId.

items.title: Surface reviews (course detail + profile section)
items.rationale: Show reviews on /courses/[id]; add a 'Reviews' Section to profile (profile/page.tsx:285, the existing Section shell). Endpoints: GET …/reviews, GET /api/users/{id}/reviews.

items.title: Course discovery from home (recent + nearby)
items.rationale: One quiet /courses entry point from home; recent (getRecentCourses) + nearby (searchNearby, golf-api.ts:733). Keep home calm — one affordance, not a grid.

items.headless: backend tests yes; full path device
items.title: Unify course identity (GolfAPI / scoring_courses / mapped PostGIS)
items.rationale: Reconcile the 4 course systems so reviews + GPS + scoring share one course id. Already flagged in specs/real-data-wiring-plan.md. Touches supabase/migrations (guard-protected — owner applies). Sequence LAST; B1–B4 deliberately avoid depending on it by keying reviews on golfApiCourseId.

ui_decision_social_course: NO global bottom tab bar. NORTHSTAR forbids SaaS/dashboard chrome; the app is intentionally hub-and-spoke (calm home, full-bleed sub-screens that return to /). Neither feature is a 'camp here' destination — Partners are contextual (adding/inviting players), course search is already mid-flow in round-setup & tee-time. Homes instead: Playing Partners = promote the orphaned /players page + quiet entry from home/profile and inline in player pickers; Courses = keep search where it lives + ONE quiet /courses spoke from home with /courses/[id] detail. If the owner ever insists on persistent nav, the only Northstar-compatible form is a 3-item home dock (Round·Partners·Courses) shown ONLY on home — but recommendation is to not build it.
